reason_for_decision: The article passes because it explains TAS and TWS as different scheduling workloads, not as directly comparable capacity badges, which is exactly the distinction readers need.
strengths: The article handles the acronym ambiguity around TAS carefully without losing practical usefulness.
It is strong on why capacity numbers do not convert one-to-one across TWS and TAS workloads.
The links between scan architecture, update rhythm, and target capacity give the article real engineering depth.
summary_assessment: This is a high-quality pass. The article is technically careful, operationally useful, and unusually strong at explaining why published TAS and TWS figures are often misread.
